title: F0R3V3R50F7's LCE ReTuned
description: A fork of Re-Console, tuned and configured for maximum performance with zero-compromises.
body:
# F0R3V3R50F7's LCE ReTuned | A fork of Re-Console

![Gameplay](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/F0R3V3R50F7/lce-retuned/refs/heads/main/2025-05-24_00-10-31.png)

![Menu](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/F0R3V3R50F7/lce-retuned/refs/heads/main/2025-05-24_00-11-30.png)

## Why another Legacy Console Modpack? 

After testing a dozen modpacks created for exactly the same purpose, I'm very frustrated with the selection. Too many promise to run on low-end hardware and present us with a slideshow. Some are missing features like the LCE Texture Packs, Tutorial Worlds and some basic fixes/mods I would reguard as essential. I created this pack to share what I believe to be the definitive LCE experience for absolute potatoes with absolutely zero compromises.

This is Minecraft as it should be enjoyed. Elegant, user-friendly and carefully configured for super-speed! This pack was created on a laptop over 10 years old with a dual core 2GHz i3 CPU. It is configured to stick as close to 60fps as possible at all times.

I will release a "quantum computer" version in the coming months with culling, fog, render distance and such cranked. I will also be building this pack for newer/older versions of the game. They will all be updated and maintained as their own branches. Please be patient.

Yes, I am aware of the Vulkan mod. Right now, there seems to be some compaatibility issues with some of the mods I'm using in this pack. I will look into this issue soon and attempt a vulkan build.

## Installation

* Prism Launcher

1.  Select "Add Instance" in the top left corner of the launcher.
2.  Select "Modrinth" in the side-bar.
3.  Type "LCE ReTuned" in the search box.
4.  Select "F0R3V3R50F7's LCE ReTuned" in the results and hit ok. Now the modpack should begin installation.

* World Host
